title: Squid Delay Pool Throttling Signals
app: Splunk Universal Forwarder, Splunk Connect for Syslog, or HTTP Event Collector; `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` for listed sourcetypes
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="squid:cache"
| regex _raw="(?i)Delay pool|delay_pool"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count by host, _time
description: Fair-use rules should be observable, not silent.
implementation: Avoid verbose debug in prod; use short cache log notices or manager counters.
visualization: Time series for rates and latency, top/dedup for hotspots, single-value alerts on health thresholds.
grandmaExplanation: We watch squid delay pool throttling signals and catch issues early, before they turn into outages for the people who rely on the network.
knownFalsePositives: Edge cases: multi-vendor mixes and ad hoc feeds can include lab traffic and partial visibility that mimics issues until scopes are defined. Tuning tip: match this to «Squid Delay Pool Throttling Signals» and exclude change windows, scans, and lab VLANs you already expect.
